text: She is a big old brown dog.
He is a small noisy young one.
After the walk, I am exhausted.
That is because the puppy is exhausting.
You mentioned two dogs. What are they actually like?
Completely different. Mel is a big old brown one. Very calm, very lazy, and gentle with everybody.
A small noisy young one. Playful, curious, stubborn, and honestly exhausting. I am always tired.
You said Mel was a stray. How bad was she when you found her?
Skinny, filthy and completely frightened. Now she owns the sofa and complains if dinner is late.
She is a big old brown dog, and she is very calm.
The young one is playful, curious and stubborn.
After the walk I am exhausted, because he is exhausting.
When I found her, she was skinny and frightened.
She is gentle with everybody, even with strangers.

She was the most frightened animal I have ever seen. Skinny, filthy, hiding under a parked car, and she would not come out for anything. It took me three days and a lot of cheap sausages. Now, four years later, she is a big, lazy, ridiculous creature who sleeps on my sofa and complains if the food is late. People say rescue dogs are grateful. I do not know about grateful. I think she just decided that this was hers now, and honestly, she was right. The house is hers. I only pay for it.

The morning walk is not relaxing, whatever people tell you. One of them is old and slow and wants to smell every single thing, and the other one is young and completely exhausting. So I am being pulled in two directions for forty minutes. But there is a moment, usually around the corner by the field, when they both stop at the same time and just look at something. A bird, or nothing at all. And it is very quiet, and I am not thinking about anything. That is the moment I go out for.

First, Tom asks Ana what her dogs are like. Then Ana describes Mel, the older one, as calm and lazy. Next, she says the younger one is noisy and exhausting. After that, she explains how Mel was frightened when she found her. Finally, she admits that the dogs decided the house was theirs.
She is a big old brown dog.
After the walk I am exhausted.
When I found her she was frightened.
He refuses to move. He is so stubborn.
She is a big old brown dog, and she is very calm.
The young one is playful, curious and stubborn.
After the walk I am exhausted, because he is exhausting.
When I found her, she was skinny and frightened.
She is gentle with everybody, even with strangers.
